channels.label: Stable
channels.summary: Diagnostic hardening point for the tamamono 1.x line, with a connected system install-policy surface, workflow diagnostics plane, preview-first validation and repair flows, and repository-wide 1.6.0 version alignment across desktop shells, release metadata, and packaged docs.
notes: Primary operator-facing update track for the current shipping line.
Targets standard installs that follow the visible installation contract.

visible_rules.label: preflight integrity
visible_rules.description: Run installation integrity before switching or repairing the current release.

visible_rules.label: layout repair
visible_rules.description: Update handoff may recreate missing standard roots before applying the target release.

visible_rules.label: residue cleanup
visible_rules.description: Only residue approved by the installation integrity contract may be removed automatically.

visible_rules.label: rollback posture
visible_rules.description: Rollback stays explicit and visible by reinstalling a concrete shipped version from the same channel.

notes: System install policy can now jump into Workflow Builder targets and trigger safe preview or scan actions
Workflow Builder now groups validation, component integrity, and package-install maintenance into one diagnostics plane
Validation and package maintenance both expose preview-first panels instead of burying repair intent inside plain list rows
Hub shell, packaged docs, and release-facing version badges now report the tamamono 1.6.0 shipping line
Workbench desktop shell and bundled assets now align with the 1.6.0 line and visible product-version contract
Installer update and integrity panels now speak the 1.6.0 contract and keep visible version alignment with release metadata
Shared workflow metadata defaults, language-pack defaults, and release-facing update catalogs now align to 1.6.0

versions.summary: Diagnostic hardening point for the tamamono 1.x line, with a connected system install-policy surface, workflow diagnostics plane, preview-first validation and repair flows, and repository-wide 1.6.0 version alignment across desktop shells, release metadata, and packaged docs.

notes: System install policy can now jump into Workflow Builder targets and trigger safe preview or scan actions
Workflow Builder now groups validation, component integrity, and package-install maintenance into one diagnostics plane
Validation and package maintenance both expose preview-first panels instead of burying repair intent inside plain list rows
Hub shell, packaged docs, and release-facing version badges now report the tamamono 1.6.0 shipping line
Workbench desktop shell and bundled assets now align with the 1.6.0 line and visible product-version contract
Installer update and integrity panels now speak the 1.6.0 contract and keep visible version alignment with release metadata
Shared workflow metadata defaults, language-pack defaults, and release-facing update catalogs now align to 1.6.0

versions.summary: Industrial hardening point for the tamamono 1.x line, with richer workflow authoring, operator descriptor APIs, Blender-leaning workbench styling, responsive layout validation, and desktop bundles rebuilt as 1.4.0.

notes: Graphite default theme with Blender-like darker industrial panel language
Responsive layout guard and Playwright UI overlap/overflow validation
Workflow Builder supports topology, dataset, interface, draft storage, local workflow library, lineage, notes, and draft execution
Docs entrypoints now open packaged HTML docs instead of local markdown files
Desktop shell rebuilt after workflow and UI responsiveness updates
Desktop installer shell rebuilt in line with current version chain
Operator descriptor endpoints exposed at /api/v1/operators and /api/v1/operators/:operator_id
Workflow graph synchronous and async execution APIs available
Named workflow catalog API available
